crime.value: Hit-and-run vehicular homicide using a semi truck to kill three men at a wrecking yard, disguised as an accident
crime.quote: the trailer drives up and over the 66 pontiac GTO, the driver keeps on driving

victim.value: Ray Richardson (primary target) who was embezzling money from Richard; Jack London and Mike London were unintended victims
victim.quote: you found out that ray was ripping you off, he owed you lots of money

closed_world.value: Small city of Victor Idaho and connected locations (New York City, Butte Montana) with limited suspects
closed_world.quote: This small city of Victor Idaho

culprit_and_motive.value: Richard (Jack's brother, east coast mobster) killed Ray Richardson for embezzling money from him
culprit_and_motive.quote: I'm arresting you, for the murder of Jack London, Mike London, and Ray Richardson

resolution.value: Detective John Lewis solves the case through investigation, linking Richard to the truck purchase and disguise elements
resolution.quote: you bought a black ken worth in Butte Montana, paid cash for it, used the name Mike Henderson to buy it

investigator.value: Detective John Lewis, an official police detective with institutional authority investigating the hit-and-run

suspect_architecture.value: Primary suspect Richard (east coast mobster brother), with victims Jack London (lawyer), Mike London, Ray Richardson (con-man who owed money). Richard has clear motive (financial betrayal), means (resources to buy truck/equipment), and opportunity (traveled to scene).
suspect_architecture.quote: Richard the big time east coast mobster...ray was ripping you off, he owed you lots of money

red_herring.value: The supernatural devil/demonic truck driver with glowing red eyes and 'I have come for you' mud flaps, designed to misdirect from the actual human perpetrator using theatrical props.
red_herring.quote: black ken worth ,with eerie red glowing head lights. The driver has red glowing eyes, and a black hood

clue_fairness.value: Key evidence is presented to reader: cash purchase under false name Mike Henderson, stolen trailers, custom glowing headlights from Tom Howard, red contact lenses, and Richard's overly helpful behavior during investigation.
clue_fairness.quote: used the name Mike Henderson to buy it,riped off two semi trailers, went to Tom Howard

social_world.value: Small town Victor Idaho police department with clear hierarchy (Police Chief Sam, Detective John), mayor oversight, and connections to big city organized crime through Richard's east coast mob ties.
social_world.quote: the police chief Sam Croft ford...Ted the mayor...richard the big time east coast mobster

alibi.value: Richard's false alibi of being uninvolved collapses when John proves he was 'too helpful' during the investigation and traces the truck purchase, equipment modifications, and financial motive.
alibi.quote: you played like, you didn't know what happen, you even acted like you, were helping me out

reveal_mechanic.value: John demonstrates the solution through reconstructing Richard's elaborate preparation: truck purchase under false identity, trailer theft, custom prop creation (glowing lights/contacts), establishing the staged supernatural appearance.
reveal_mechanic.quote: bought a black ken worth in Butte Montana, paid cash for it, used the name Mike Henderson

media_and_audience.value: The crime is initially perceived through supernatural lens - witnesses and police chief reference 'the devil' driving the truck, creating public narrative of demonic intervention rather than calculated murder.
media_and_audience.quote: what don't tell me that, the devil was driving the semi truck, get out of my office

_meta.title: ebook james mad monk death comes
